title: Gartner, Inc. (IT) — Intrinsic Value & Prospect Score
description: An independent discounted cash flow (DCF) intrinsic value analysis of Gartner (IT) compiled by Gemini. Economic Prospect Score: 86/100 — Strong Prospect.

fcfGrowth.title: FCF Growth Rate (Y1-Y10)
fcfGrowth.rationale: A 9% growth rate models consistent, highly predictable double-digit growth in Global Technology Sales contract value, bolstered by high retention rates and regular price increases.

discountRate.title: Discount Rate (WACC)
discountRate.rationale: An 8.5% discount rate reflects Gartner's exceptionally low business risk profile. The recurring, subscription-based revenue model and dominant market position justify a lower risk premium.

terminalGrowth.title: Terminal Growth Rate
terminalGrowth.rationale: A 3% terminal growth rate assumes Gartner will continue to grow slightly faster than overall global GDP, reflecting the permanent and increasing complexity of the technology landscape.

thesisTitle: The Toll Road for Global IT Spending
thesisParagraphs: Gartner is arguably one of the highest-quality compounders in the market today. It operates an almost perfectly resilient business model: as technology becomes more complex and confusing (accelerated recently by generative AI), the demand for Gartner's proprietary, independent analysis increases. They provide the ultimate 'CYA' (Cover Your Assets) service for the C-suite—no CIO gets fired for buying the vendor in the top right of the Gartner Magic Quadrant. This dynamic grants them immense pricing power and client retention rates that rival the best enterprise software companies.

The valuation presented by the DCF model highlights a massive discrepancy. The current market price fails to adequately reflect the staggering amount of free cash flow Gartner produces relative to its capital-light needs. When combined with a management team that ruthlessly and efficiently uses that cash to retire outstanding shares, the intrinsic value per share compounding is exceptional. At current levels, Gartner represents a significant value dislocation and an asymmetrical investment opportunity.
verdictFooter: Based on recent financial actuals. Data via S&P Global.

faq.question: Why is the margin of safety for Gartner so exceptionally high?
faq.answer: The model indicates that the market is severely underpricing Gartner's highly predictable, recurring free cash flow stream and the compounding effect of its aggressive share buyback program. The $159.26 price implies near-zero future growth, which contradicts the company's historical and projected performance.

faq.question: What is the biggest risk to Gartner's business model?
faq.answer: The biggest risk is reputational. If enterprise buyers lose faith in the objectivity of Gartner's research (e.g., believing vendors can buy their way into favorable Magic Quadrant positions), the core moat would collapse.

faq.question: How does AI impact Gartner's intrinsic value?
faq.answer: AI acts as a massive catalyst. The confusion and rapid pace of AI development drive C-suite executives directly to Gartner for guidance on procurement and strategy, ensuring high demand and pricing power for their advisory services over the next decade.

prospectVerdictDetail: Gartner operates a highly lucrative, subscription-based business model that effectively acts as a toll road for global IT spending. As enterprise technology becomes increasingly complex (particularly with the rise of AI), the C-suite's reliance on Gartner's independent research and advisory services deepens. This dynamic yields exceptional pricing power, massive free cash flow generation, and high revenue retention. Management's commitment to aggressive share repurchases further compounds intrinsic value per share.

competitiveMomentum.title: Competitive Momentum
competitiveMomentum.summary: Gartner exhibits exceptional momentum as the undeniable industry standard for enterprise technology research and advisory.

factors.name: Revenue Growth vs. Peers
factors.rationale: While top-line revenue growth is steady rather than explosive, it is highly predictable and compounded by margin expansion. Contract Value (CV) growth consistently trends in the high single to low double digits.

factors.name: Market Share Trajectory
factors.rationale: Gartner holds a commanding and expanding market share. Its 'Magic Quadrant' is the de facto benchmark in the IT industry, making it essentially a mandatory subscription for major enterprise vendors and buyers.

factors.name: Pricing Power
factors.rationale: Pricing power is immense. The cost of a Gartner subscription is trivial compared to the multi-million dollar IT procurement decisions it influences, allowing for consistent, high-margin price increases.

factors.name: Product Velocity
factors.rationale: Velocity is demonstrated through the continuous coverage of emerging technologies. The rapid integration of AI analysis into their research portfolio ensures the service remains critically relevant to the C-suite.

moatDurability.title: Moat Durability
moatDurability.summary: Gartner's moat is extraordinarily wide, built on network effects, a vast proprietary data repository, and immense brand authority.

factors.name: Switching Costs
factors.rationale: For vendors, being dropped from Gartner's coverage is commercially devastating. For enterprise buyers, switching to a lesser-known advisory firm introduces significant career risk when making major procurement decisions.

factors.name: Network Effects
factors.rationale: A powerful two-sided network effect exists: the more enterprises rely on Gartner, the more vendors must engage with Gartner to be evaluated, generating more proprietary data that further enriches the research for buyers.

factors.name: Regulatory & IP Position
factors.rationale: Gartner's intellectual property consists of decades of proprietary data, methodologies, and thousands of expert analysts. This institutional knowledge base is functionally impossible for a new entrant to replicate quickly.

factors.name: Capital Intensity Advantage
factors.rationale: The business model is highly asset-light. Generating research requires human capital, not heavy machinery, resulting in exceptional free cash flow conversion rates.

sentimentCatalyst.title: Sentiment & Catalysts
sentimentCatalyst.summary: Market sentiment is deeply positive, underpinned by the secular complexity of enterprise IT and a shareholder-friendly capital allocation strategy.

factors.name: Earnings Estimate Revisions
factors.rationale: Estimates remain strong as enterprises continue to prioritize IT spending and rely on advisory services to navigate complex digital transformations and AI implementations.

factors.name: News & Narrative Sentiment
factors.rationale: The narrative recognizes Gartner as a definitive 'picks and shovels' play on the broader technology sector. Regardless of which tech vendor wins, Gartner gets paid to analyze the race.

factors.name: Management & Capital Allocation
factors.rationale: Management employs a masterful, highly accretive capital allocation strategy, utilizing massive free cash flow generation to consistently and aggressively repurchase shares, driving strong EPS growth.

prospectKeyRisks: A severe, prolonged reduction in global enterprise IT budgets could lead to delayed renewals or downsizing of subscription tiers.
Reputational damage regarding the objectivity of its research (e.g., accusations of 'pay-to-play' dynamics with vendors) could permanently impair its core moat.
The rise of advanced AI models could eventually commoditize basic technical research, forcing Gartner to pivot entirely to highly bespoke, high-level strategic advisory.
prospectKeyCatalysts: The rapid proliferation of enterprise AI technologies, driving an intense need for independent guidance and spurring new, high-margin research subscriptions.
Continued aggressive share repurchase programs that significantly reduce the outstanding share count and artificially boost earnings per share.
Expansion into non-IT functional areas (e.g., HR, Finance, Supply Chain), increasing the total addressable market within existing enterprise clients.
prospectMethodology: Consensus Analysis — Economic Prospect Score averaging independent evaluations from Opus 4.6 and Gemini 3.1. Gemini scored IT at 83/100 and Opus at 86/100. Each factor score is the arithmetic mean of both models. Three pillars: Competitive Momentum (0-35), Moat Durability (0-35), and Sentiment & Catalysts (0-30).
